medical_history.chief_complaint: Acute-onset consciousness disturbance, fever, and elevated serum creatinine kinase levels
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Acute-onset consciousness disturbance (a Japanese coma scale score of 200), fever, and elevated serum creatinine kinase levels in mid-September 2010. Convulsion was noted in late-September. Treatment with intravenous acyclovir and methylprednisolone pulse therapy was partially effective. The patient continued to have dementia with myoclonus and psychiatric symptoms despite resolution of the consciousness disturbance.
medical_history.past_medical_history: No medical or familial history of central nervous system disease

physical_examination.general_examination: Disturbed consciousness gradually improved, and myoclonus was observed in his right upper limb. Dementia, psychiatric symptoms, a startle reaction, myoclonus, truncal and limb rigidity, and spasticity in all four limbs were noted. Muscle weakness, atrophy, and fasciculation were not observed.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological examination showed dementia, psychiatric symptoms, a startle reaction, myoclonus, truncal and limb rigidity, and spasticity in all four limbs. Periodic sharp-wave complexes were not observed on the serial electroencephalograms.
